channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Melbourne Football Club and CEO Paul Guerra mutually agree to part ways
item.description: Melbourne Football Club has announced that it will be parting ways with chief executive Paul Guerra, just seven months after he officially took on the role. Guerra was appointed in April 2025 and bega...

item.title: Animal rights groups question McLaren Circus permits amid city approval
item.description: The McLaren Circus, currently stationed in Milnerton for its annual tour, is facing criticism from animal rights organisations over the use of wild animals in captivity. Beauty Without Cruelty (BWC) h...

item.title: Migori County Launches KSh 110,000 Cash Transfer to Expectant Mothers to Cut Maternal Deaths
item.description: Migori County in Kenya has for long struggled with high maternal mortality rates due to home births, poverty and lack of access to proper healthcare. Things are now changing thanks to a new initiative...

item.title: NMEP sensitises FCT residents on malaria prevention ahead of World Malaria Day
item.description: The National Malaria Eradication Programme (NMEP), under the Federal Ministry of Health and Social Welfare, waka through the city centre of Abuja yesterday dey sensitise residents on how dem fit help ...

item.title: King Charles begins first state visit to the U.S. amidst strained UK-US relations
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived in Washington, D.C., for a four-day state visit hosted by U.S. President Donald Trump. The visit comes at a critical time in UK-U.S. relations, which have de...

item.title: Billy Porter reveals he has not been invited back to the Met Gala since 2019
item.description: Billy Porter, the acclaimed actor known for his role in 'Pose', revealed in a recent interview that he has not been invited to the Met Gala since his striking debut in 2019. At that event, themed 'Cam...

item.title: How Druski Built a $14 Million Media Empire Through Comedy and Music
item.description: Comedian Drew Desbordes, popularly known as Druski, has turned his passion for comedy into a booming media empire, earning a remarkable $14 million in 2025 alone. Starting off as a broke college stude...

item.title: USIU-Africa Advertises Several Senior Job Vacancies Including Head of Security and Procurement
item.description: United States International University-Africa (USIU-Africa) has announced several job vacancies for senior leadership and academic positions to strengthen its operations. The positions include Manager...

item.title: Sub-Saharan Africa: HIV Testing Rates Among Young Women Remain a Critical Concern
item.description: A recent article highlights the ongoing HIV epidemic disproportionately affecting adolescent girls and young women in sub-Saharan Africa. Researchers emphasize that ensuring these young women know the...

item.title: Funeral Held for Monswamy Family Following KwaZulu-Natal Killings
item.description: The tragic murder of Allan Monswamy, his wife Sandy, their children Kraidon and Shamarie, and three relatives in KwaDukuza has left the KwaZulu-Natal community in deep shock. A mass funeral for the se...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs coach Kalil Ben Youssef criticises pre-match scuffle involving Orlando Pirates in Soweto derby
item.description: In the highly anticipated Soweto derby at FNB Stadium on Sunday, Kaizer Chiefs and Orlando Pirates played to a 1-1 draw, with goals from Pule Mmodi for Chiefs and Kamogelo Sebelebele for Pirates in th...

item.title: Legal challenges in the proposed merger of seven AAP MPs with BJP
item.description: On 26th April 2026, Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) leader Sanjay Singh filed a petition with the Rajya Sabha Chairperson seeking disqualification of seven AAP MPs who announced their merger with the Bharatiya ...

item.title: Mombasa: DCI Probes Death of Indian-Origin Man Found in Bamburi Short-Stay Apartment
item.description: Detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) have launched probes into the mysterious death of a middle-aged man of Indian origin in Mombasa County. The incident happened in a shor...

item.title: Ramaphosa reaffirms government's commitment to fight corruption and crime at Freedom Day
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa delivered a keynote address at the Dr Rantlai Molemela Stadium in Bloemfontein, emphasising the South African government's ongoing efforts to combat corruption, organised cri...

item.title: Diesel and Kerosene Prices Drop While Gasoline Slightly Increases as of April 28
item.description: Fuel prices in the Philippines continue to fluctuate as oil companies implement new adjustments effective Tuesday, April 28. This marks the third straight week of price changes, largely influenced by ...

item.title: Narok Family Mourns Loss of Clinician in Tragic Mai Mahiu Road Accident
item.description: A Narok family is reeling from shock after discovering that their relative, a clinical officer, was among eight people who tragically lost their lives in a head-on collision on the Mai Mahiu–Narok hig...

item.title: GO Transit to introduce faster express train service between Kitchener and Toronto
item.description: New express GO Train service is being introduced to improve travel between Kitchener and Toronto, offering commuters faster and more convenient transit options along one of southwestern Ontario’s busi...

item.title: IMF Report Highlights Economic Risks of Military Spending and Conflict
item.description: The International Monetary Fund’s April 2026 World Economic Outlook, titled 'Global Economy in the Shadow of War', examines the economic impact of rising defence spending and global conflicts. It note...

item.title: Manchester United Near Champions League Qualification After Win Over Brentford
item.description: Manchester United don move closer to securing Champions League football after dem beat Brentford 2-1 for Old Trafford. Casemiro open the scoring early in the match with one fine header from set-piece,...

item.title: MPs Raise Concerns Over Future of AUKUS Defence Agreement
item.description: Concerns have been raised by Members of Parliament regarding the stability of the AUKUS defence pact, a landmark agreement between the United Kingdom, the United States, and Australia. While details a...
